name: Email Production Pipeline v2 - Chat + Image + Gemini

parameters.content:
# Email Production Pipeline v2

## Two entry points:
- **Chat UI** — paste HTML, upload design images, give feedback interactively
- **Form** — structured input with all fields, good for handoffs

## Setup Required:
1. Add **Google Cloud (Service Account)** credentials for Vertex AI Gemini
2. Configure your GCP **Project ID** in both Vertex model nodes
3. Optionally configure SMTP, Jira, MarTech credentials for delivery

## How it works:
1. Submit via chat or form
2. Agent processes HTML or rebuilds from image
3. Agent applies POUR accessibility + dark mode
4. You review and give feedback in chat
5. Agent runs automated tests
6. When approved → packages for delivery

## Chat Entry Point
Users interact conversationally:
- Paste Figma HTML
- Upload design images (Gemini vision)
- Share image URLs
- Provide link mappings
- Give iterative feedback
- Approve for delivery

## Form Entry Point
Structured input with fields for:
- Campaign name
- HTML paste or image URL
- Link mappings
- Image asset URLs
- Brand guidelines
- Proof email + Jira project

Form data gets formatted and
fed to the same AI Agent.

## Agent Tools

**Run Tests** — Automated accessibility,
dark mode, heading hierarchy, alt text,
font size, link quality, and email
best practice checks.

**Package Delivery** — Formats the final
HTML + test report + brand notes into
structured output for email proof,
Jira ticket, and MarTech push.

## Delivery (Configure These)

After the agent packages delivery,
you can add downstream nodes:

1. **Send Email** node → SMTP creds
2. **HTTP Request** → MarTech API
3. **Jira** node → Jira Cloud creds

Connect them to the agent's output
for automated delivery after approval.

The agent will tell you when the
package is ready.

## Google Vertex AI Setup

1. Go to GCP Console → IAM → Service Accounts
2. Create a service account with **Vertex AI User** role
3. Download the JSON key file
4. In n8n: Settings → Credentials → New → Google Cloud (Service Account)
5. Paste the JSON key contents
6. Set your **Project ID** in the model nodes below
7. Enable the **Vertex AI API** in your GCP project

Model: gemini-3.1-pro (multimodal, high quality)

parameters.formDescription: Submit a design for POUR-accessible email HTML production. You can provide either Figma HTML export or a design image URL.

values.placeholder: Paste the complete HTML from DanHTML Figma export here (if using HTML input)...

Header CTA → https://...
Footer link → https://...
Logo → https://...

Logo: https://...
Hero image: https://...
Product image: https://...

Primary color: #1A2B3C
Font: Inter
Spacing rules...
Tone of voice...

assignments.name: chatInput

options.systemMessage: You are an expert email production assistant working in a creative agency. You help users transform design inputs into clean, accessible, production-ready HTML emails. # YOUR CAPABILITIES 1. **Accept Figma HTML exports** — Clean up bloated Figma-generated HTML/CSS into email-client-compatible code 2. **Accept design images** — When a user shares a screenshot, mockup, or image URL, analyze it visually and rebuild it as clean HTML email code 3. **Apply POUR accessibility** — Make all output fully WCAG accessible 4. **Support dark mode** — Include proper dark mode CSS with Outlook compatibility 5. **Validate against brand guidelines** — When provided, check colors, fonts, spacing, and tone 6. **Run automated tests** — Use the `run_accessibility_tests` tool to validate your HTML 7. **Package for delivery** — Use the `package_for_delivery` tool to format output for proof emails, Jira tickets, and MarTech platforms # WORKFLOW 1. When a user provides input (HTML paste, image, or form submission), acknowledge what you received 2. Process it into clean email HTML following ALL rules below 3. Present the complete HTML to the user in a code block 4. Ask if they want changes — iterate based on feedback 5. When the user is satisfied, run the `run_accessibility_tests` tool on the final HTML 6. Share test results and fix any issues 7. When the user says "approved" or "looks good" or "ship it", use the `package_for_delivery` tool # EMAIL HTML BEST PRACTICES - Use table-based layout for maximum email client compatibility (Gmail, Outlook, Apple Mail, Yahoo) - Inline all critical CSS styles — email clients strip

parameters.name: run_accessibility_tests
parameters.description: Run automated accessibility, dark mode, and email best practice tests on HTML email code. Pass the complete HTML string as input. Returns a detailed test report with passed checks, issues, and warnings.

parameters.name: package_for_delivery
parameters.description: Package the final approved HTML email for delivery. Pass a JSON string with these fields: html (the final HTML), campaignName, proofEmail, jiraProject, brandNotes, testSummary. Returns formatted delivery packages for email proof, Jira ticket, and MarTech push.
